title: Your Stories Q&A: Dispute over repairing potholes leads to DeWitt, Salina road being closed
summary: A dispute over road maintenance responsibility between Town of Salina, Town of DeWitt, and NYSDOT regarding a deteriorated 0.1-mile stretch of Townline Road has resulted in Salina closing and abandoning the road. The incident highlights intergovernmental coordination failures and unclear jurisdiction over boundary-area infrastructure, with potential implications for similar edge-case municipal assets.

entity_refs.name: New York State Department of Transportation

political_signal.description: Public complaint escalation and media investigation exposed intergovernmental finger-pointing over maintenance responsibility, resulting in road closure. Demonstrates accountability gaps in boundary-area infrastructure jurisdiction.

processing_notes: Article is from adjacent municipality (Salina/DeWitt border) but relevant to Syracuse CIO for intergovernmental coordination and asset management system insights
No direct Syracuse City departments involved; value is cautionary/procedural for similar boundary disputes
PROVISIONAL TAG APPLIED: 'road-closure-fees' considered but not applied—closure appears administrative/punitive rather than fee-generating
Suggests need for GIS/asset inventory system validation across municipal boundaries
